message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Adding opt-in debug/trace logging to StreamListener (start/end detection, flush, reset logs). Only touching this one file. What are you working on?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/messages.py
note: I'm implementing idle_timeout_s/tick() in streaming_listener.py. I need to set is_last_chunk=True on the timeout-emitted StreamResponse if that field exists (feature1). I'll do this defensively (e.g. try/setattr or hasattr check) inside streaming_listener.py so you don't need to touch it - please add the is_last_chunk field itself to StreamResponse in messages.py and wire it up elsewhere. Let me know if you also need to edit streaming_listener.py's receive()/flush() - if so we should split by method region instead.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/messages.py
note: My feature is specifically debug/trace logging inside StreamListener itself (new __init__ params debug/debug_logger, and guarded log.debug(...) calls at: start-detection point, rolling-end-check point, emit-chunk point in receive(), flush(), and reset). I can't implement it by only touching messages.py - I have to touch streaming_listener.py too. To avoid collisions: I'll only ADD guarded logging statements (no control-flow changes) plus two new __init__ params appended after your idle_timeout_s param. I won't touch tick() or your is_last_chunk logic at all. Please add your tick()/idle_timeout_s code as clearly separate additions (e.g. new method, own line region) so our diffs don't overlap on the same lines. I won't touch messages.py. OK to proceed on this basis?

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: We both need this same file - let's split by region instead of by file. My feature (idle timeout/tick): (1) two new instance attrs appended right after 'self.allow_reuse = allow_reuse' in __init__ (self.idle_timeout_s, self._last_chunk_ts), (2) ONE line inserted in receive() right after the 'if chunk_message is None: return' check, to stamp self._last_chunk_ts on non-empty chunks, plus resetting that stamp in the existing allow_reuse reset block (lines ~86-95), (3) a brand-new tick(now_ts=None) method appended AFTER flush() (before the module-level find_predictor_for_stream_listeners function). Please keep your debug/trace logging additions away from those specific insertion points (e.g. don't add log lines between the None-check and the start-identifier logic, and don't insert a method between flush() and find_predictor_for_stream_listeners) - anywhere else in receive()/flush()/__init__ is fine for your logging. Reply ACCEPT if workable.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Agreed. I'll add idle_timeout_s + self._last_chunk_ts right after 'self.allow_reuse = allow_reuse' in __init__; you append your debug/debug_logger params right after mine. I'll add one line in receive() right after the None-check to stamp _last_chunk_ts, and reset it in the existing allow_reuse reset block - please keep guarded log calls elsewhere (not on that exact same line). My new tick() method goes after flush(), before find_predictor_for_stream_listeners - you won't touch tick(). Proceeding to implement now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Accepted. To avoid literal same-line collisions: in the __init__ signature I'll add my params (debug: bool = False, debug_logger: logging.Logger | None = None) AFTER your idle_timeout_s param. In the body I'll add self._logger = debug_logger or logging.getLogger(...) AFTER your self.idle_timeout_s/self._last_chunk_ts lines (still before self.adapter_identifiers = {...}). I'll add my start/rolling-end-check log lines inline near those checks in receive() (not touching your None-check or reset-block lines), my emit-chunk log right before 'return StreamResponse(...)', my flush log inside flush() (not touching its return logic), and my reset log inside the existing allow_reuse reset block in receive() as an added line (not modifying your reset lines, just adding one log.debug call alongside). I won't add any new method between flush() and find_predictor_for_stream_listeners - that stays yours for tick(). Proceeding now.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Finished: idle_timeout_s param, self._last_chunk_ts tracking in receive() (stamped after the None-check, reset in the allow_reuse reset block), and a new tick(now_ts=None) method appended right after flush() (before find_predictor_for_stream_listeners). tick() emits StreamResponse(..., chunk='') and unconditionally resets state on timeout, and sets is_last_chunk=True on the response only if that field already exists on StreamResponse (via dataclasses.fields check) so it's forward-compatible with your/other feature1 work without requiring changes on my end. Existing test suite (tests/streaming/test_streaming.py) passes. All clear for you to add your debug logging on top.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Finished: debug/debug_logger __init__ params (added right after allow_reuse), guarded self._logger.debug(...) trace calls for start detection (in the concat_message-matches-start_identifier branch), rolling end check (right before self.stream_end=True), emit chunk (right before 'return StreamResponse(...)'), flush() (captured return value into 'truncated' var, logged, then returned), and reset (inside the existing allow_reuse reset block, added as an extra line, didn't touch your reset lines). Did not touch tick() or the None-check line. All existing tests in tests/streaming/ pass (15 passed, 3 skipped).
